semantic_journey: Arabic dakka = to crush, bury, level flat. Greek adikekei = has wronged/done injustice. Physical crushing and doing-injustice require 3+ steps: crush -> harm -> cause-harm -> wrong-someone. Different domains.
key_insight: Arabic crushing/leveling (physical) and Greek doing-injustice (moral/legal) require 3+ steps to connect.

semantic_journey: Arabic daha = to spread out, open out. Greek andikha = asunder, in twain (directional adverb from anti+dikha). Spreading-out and the asunder-adverb are adjacent but andikha derives from Greek morphology not from Arabic. No valid derivation path.
key_insight: Arabic spreading-out and Greek asunder-adverb are adjacent but the adverb has Greek internal morphology; no valid derivation path.

semantic_journey: Arabic al-anqash = emaciation, loss of flesh. Greek klinikos = of/for a bed-patient (kline=bed + -ikos). Emaciation and bed-patient are related but 3+ steps apart: emaciation -> weakness -> unable-to-walk -> bed-confined.
key_insight: Arabic emaciation and Greek bed-patient/clinical are related but 3+ semantic steps apart.

semantic_journey: Arabic al-qandhaw = calamities (same root as GRC_0642). Greek olekonto = were being destroyed/perishing. Calamity -> destruction -> perishing is a 2-step chain: calamity-event -> experiencing-destruction -> perishing. Borderline at low confidence.
key_insight: Arabic calamities and Greek being-destroyed/perishing are 2 steps apart; borderline plausible at low confidence.

semantic_journey: Arabic shakhasa = visible irregularity, things jutting apart. Greek aiskesi = shame, ugliness (dative plural of aiskhos). Irregularity/divergence and shame/ugliness are adjacent in negative-valuation but distinct. Getting from irregularity to shame requires 3 steps.
key_insight: Arabic visible irregularity and Greek shame/ugliness are adjacent but distinct requiring 3+ steps to connect.

semantic_journey: Arabic fashat = to spread, diffuse (of disease or news). Greek paraphyas = a side-growth, lateral shoot. Core: lateral spreading. 1-step action-to-result: spreading-outward -> side-growth as botanical result of spreading.
key_insight: Arabic spreading/diffusion and Greek side-shoot share lateral spreading; Arabic is the action, Greek is the botanical result.

semantic_journey: Arabic shanaqa = to hang by cord, to strangle/bind. Greek synoikizo = to make people live together, to settle together. Binding/hanging and cohabitation are in entirely different semantic domains with no valid 2-step path.
key_insight: Arabic binding/hanging-with-cord and Greek making-people-live-together are entirely different semantic domains.

semantic_journey: Arabic al-karath = a calamitous or harmful matter. Greek okhlokratia = mob rule. Both negative social phenomena but 3+ steps apart: harmful-matter -> social-harm -> social-chaos -> mob-rule.
key_insight: Arabic calamitous matter and Greek mob-rule are both negative but 3+ semantic steps apart.
